theme: AI washing, false AI claims, and human-as-AI cases
purpose: Resolve the highest-value remaining shard-009 FTC hold states that currently block reviewer-safe use of Rytr and Air.ai in the LIMEN paper package.
why_now: The hostile-reviewer sprint favors exact claim support and queue retirement over more exploratory harvesting.
Full-case review priority actions explicitly hold Rytr and Air.ai until complaint/order/set-aside documents are read.

This host can verify public FTC page existence but FTC legal-library pages are currently blocked to local shell and browser retrieval, so a bounded follow-through request is the smallest publishability move available without unsafe access workarounds.

current_blocking_evidence.fact: Rytr and Air.ai are held until primary complaint/order/set-aside materials are read.
Rytr is a complex-posture hold; Air.ai is a document-read-pending hold.
Public FTC case pages were visible via web search/open, but local shell curl returned 403 and no bypass was attempted.

current_blocking_evidence.locator: browser_console on FTC Rytr case page
current_blocking_evidence.fact: FTC returned an anti-automation apology page stating the request resembled an abusive automated request; this cycle did not proceed further against the blocked surface.

targets.canonical_label: FTC Rytr AI review/testimonial generator matter with later set-aside posture
documents_needed: any commissioner statement or dissent that clarifies why the order was reopened or set aside
questions_to_answer: What exact AI-related representations are attributed to Rytr in the complaint/order text?
Does the matter fit AI washing / false AI claim, deceptive review/testimonial generation, unlawful-use enablement, or a narrower procedural/advertising posture?
What did the 2025 set-aside order actually change: liability posture, remedial posture, procedural validity, or only one part of the prior order stack?
After reading the full chronology, what is the highest safe LIMEN role: negative-results appendix, complex-posture comparator, regulator-side anchor, or not useful enough for the first paper package?
expected_outputs: one chronology table with date, document type, exact procedural state, and claim ceiling
one classification note with allowed statement / forbidden overread
one recommendation on whether Rytr stays held, moves to appendix-only complex-posture comparator, or is excluded from the first package
targets.promotion_threshold: Do not promote as a current final-order exemplar unless the document set supports that characterization after accounting for the later set-aside order.

documents_needed: final court or commission approval text if separately public
questions_to_answer: What exact AI role is alleged or described: AI marketing inflation, business-opportunity deception, human-hidden-behind-AI, or only AI-adjacent branding?
What exact product/service claims are described in the primary documents?

Does the matter belong inside shard-009 as a bounded non-finance AI-claim sidecar candidate, or is it better treated as a business-opportunity/deceptive-marketing queue outside the current core package?
What is the highest safe publication role after reading the documents: sidecar candidate, queue-only note, or excluded from first-package rhetoric?
expected_outputs: one claim-substance table with quoted/paraphrased document support
one classification note assigning the safest LIMEN category and evidence role
one recommendation on whether Air.ai remains held or becomes a bounded non-finance sidecar candidate
targets.promotion_threshold: Do not classify from the FTC page title alone; use only primary complaint/order wording plus exact procedural posture.

allowed_methods: No authentication, no form submission, no account creation, no portal access
No access-control bypass, evasion, or repeated aggressive retries
If blocked again, record the blocked state as source-state metadata rather than event evidence
paper_thesis_use: Could retire one reviewer-visible hold or at least convert it into a fully specified complex-posture appendix row.
Would strengthen the AI-washing / non-finance diversification discussion by separating promotion-ready FTC rows from merely title-level or procedurally unstable ones.
Feeds a dashboard or appendix badge layer for FTC sidecars, complex-posture candidates, and document-read-pending candidates.
visualization_dashboard_hook: FTC sidecar / hold-resolution panel with columns for target_id, procedural posture, AI-role certainty, allowed publication role, and next upgrade trigger.
success_condition: A future lane reads the primary public documents and resolves at least one of the two current hold states into a reviewer-safe role with explicit claim ceiling and chronology.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Use this request as the bounded handoff for the next public-document-reading cycle touching Rytr or Air.ai rather than reopening generic FTC discovery or title-only summaries.
